examples: If something is probable, you mean that you can trust it, so probability means trustworthiness.
A Harvard Business School study found that observers consistently rated those who were upfront about themselves more highly, while those who hid lost trustworthiness.
According to a research from Princeton University, people assess your competence, trustworthiness, and likeability in just a tenth of a second, solely based on the way you look.
As Gates defines it, "Trustworthiness" (with that capital T) is not just a matter of security.
Consumers are increasingly looking up doctor reviews, and the trustworthiness of the source is paramount.
